entities.name: Input (Systematic Listening)
entities.context: The practice of structured listening and understanding, argued to be a more defensible moat than output as AI commoditizes content generation

entities.name: Output (Content Generation)
entities.context: AI-generated content production, becoming commoditized and less defensible as a competitive advantage

entities.name: LLM
entities.context: Large Language Model whose primary professional workflow value is patient, structured ingestion rather than writing ability

entities.name: Generative AI
entities.context: Technology creating a 'writer's surplus' where high-quality output becomes commodity

entities.name: Writer's Surplus
entities.context: Condition where everyone can produce high-quality output, making output itself a commodity

entities.name: Ingestion Phase
entities.context: AI workflow phase involving giving models more context, longer transcripts, and diverse data - should be prioritized

entities.name: Prompt Engineering
entities.context: Technique for crafting AI prompts for final outputs like emails or JDs - argued to be less important than ingestion

entities.name: Defensible Moat
entities.context: Business strategy concept - input/listening becomes more defensible than output as production costs drop

entities.name: Algorithmic Curation
entities.context: Pull/ingest model that broadcast media transitioned toward

entities.name: Broadcast Media
entities.context: Push model that transitioned to algorithmic curation (pull/ingest)

relationships.context: Generative AI commoditizes content generation, reducing its value as competitive advantage
Input becomes more defensible than output as AI commoditizes production
LLM's primary professional value is capacity for patient, structured ingestion
Writer's surplus makes output a commodity since everyone can produce quality content
Workflow design should prioritize ingestion phase over prompt engineering for final outputs
Historical transition from push (broadcast) to pull/ingest (algorithmic curation) mirrors the input > output shift
